title: Bee Removal in College Point, Queens | $350-$550
metaDescription: Professional bee removal services in College Point, Queens. Safe, humane bee hive removal from homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for $350-$550 flat-rate service.
intro: College Point residents facing bee infestations need fast, professional removal services to protect their families and properties. Best Pest Control NYC offers expert bee removal throughout College Point, from the waterfront homes near Powell's Cove to the residential neighborhoods surrounding Poppenhusen Avenue. Our licensed technicians safely remove bee colonies and hives while preventing future infestations.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served College Point for years with specialized bee removal expertise, using humane relocation methods whenever possible and guaranteed results. Our technicians understand the unique challenges of Queens properties, from multi-family homes to local businesses near the College Point Shopping Center, and respond quickly to protect your property.
localContext: College Point's mix of waterfront properties, dense residential blocks, and proximity to green spaces like Flushing Airport makes it particularly susceptible to bee activity during warmer months. The area's older homes along 14th Avenue and newer developments near the MacNeil Park area both attract bees seeking shelter in eaves, chimneys, and wall voids.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove bees in College Point?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day bee removal services throughout College Point. Most bee removals are completed within 2-4 hours, depending on hive size and location. We understand that bee infestations require urgent attention and prioritize rapid response times for all College Point residents.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 bee removal service?
faqs.answer: Our flat-rate service includes complete inspection, safe bee and hive removal, treatment of the affected area, cleanup, and preventative recommendations. The final price depends on hive size, location accessibility, and whether the colony is in a wall, attic, chimney, or exterior area. We provide upfront quotes with no hidden fees.

faqs.question: Do you relocate bees or exterminate them?
faqs.answer: We prioritize humane bee relocation whenever possible, especially for honeybees which are vital pollinators. Our experts work with local beekeepers to relocate colonies when safe and feasible. In cases where aggressive species or dangerous locations pose immediate threats, we use targeted treatments that minimize environmental impact.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have a bee infestation in my College Point home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include seeing multiple bees entering and exiting a specific area of your home, hearing buzzing sounds in walls or ceilings, noticing honeycomb or wax stains, or spotting swarms around your property. College Point homes near wooded areas or the waterfront are especially prone to bee colonies in attics, soffits, and chimneys during spring and summer months.
